promo_headline: Five signals. Two moves. One pattern.
whats_news: OpenAI: Codex is moving into the phone workflow
Google: Arm-backed CPU inference is getting faster
Corey Haines: marketing skills v2.0 is a cleaner product story
Dingyi: meigen.ai is selling easy image and video output
Alex Banks: the UBI mood is turning more skeptical

digest.title: The day favors tools that reduce friction, not hype.
digest.body: The pattern today is convergence around shipping convenience: mobile control, on-device inference, cleaner packaging, and cheaper creation all remove a step between intent and output. The strongest signals point to practical adoption, not abstract AI speculation, and that is the lane Nora should track this week.

items.headline: OpenAI puts Codex on mobile, not just desktop
items.deck: OpenAI is pushing coding agents into the phone workflow so tasks can keep moving off desk. This matters because mobile approval and task handoff reduce the friction that slows long-running agent work.

items.headline: Google and Arm speed up AI on the device
items.deck: Google and Arm are showing that CPU inference can get faster without waiting for every device to add a new accelerator. This matters because cheaper on-device inference widens the deployment path for media and assistant features.

items.headline: Corey Haines renames marketing skills v2.0
items.deck: Corey Haines is simplifying his skill taxonomy into shorter names and one CRO path. This matters because cleaner product language makes the system easier to adopt and easier to expand.

items.headline: Meigen.ai leans into one-off image and video buys
items.deck: Dingyi is pitching simple paid creation workflows with prompt libraries and low per-image cost. This matters because creators adopt tools faster when pricing, prompts, and output volume are all obvious.

items.headline: Sam Altman’s UBI retreat shows the mood has shifted
items.deck: The newsletter points to a sharper, more skeptical posture on guaranteed income after years of hype. This matters because AI capital is now favoring infrastructure and shipping over broad social theory.

items.headline: Research mobile Codex workflows. Pull 3 prompts.
items.why: Mobile agent control is the clearest near-term product wedge in the day’s feed, so Nora should map the workflow now.
items.try: Set a 45-min timer. Capture three concrete mobile approval or handoff ideas.

items.headline: Draft one on-device AI angle for this week.
items.why: Google and Arm’s CPU inference push is a concrete trend that can shape Nora’s weekly angle before the market saturates it.
items.try: Set a 45-min timer. Write one publishable angle and one supporting proof point.
